items.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 Ships In-Prompt Config Syntax and Startup Fixes
items.summary: Version 2.1.181 adds /config key=value syntax for setting Claude Code settings from within prompts (interactive, -p, and Remote Control sessions). Two startup regressions fixed: ~120ms delay from managed-settings fetch blocking on missing MCP servers (introduced in 2.1.169), and up to 15-second stall when account settings fetch times out. File-truncation bug fixed for Write/Edit on network drives and cloud-synced folders (OneDrive, iCloud, Dropbox). macOS TUI freeze at session start resolved. Foreground subagents now enforce the same 5-level nesting limit as background subagents.
key_claims: /config syntax available in interactive, -p, and Remote Control
File truncation on network drives fixed
5-level foreground subagent nesting limit now enforced

items.title: Flue 1.0 Beta Ships Open-Source TypeScript Agent Harness
items.summary: Astro team released Flue 1.0 Beta on June 16: open-source TypeScript agent harness (github.com/withastro/flue, 5.3k stars). Provides sessions, tools, skills, instructions, filesystem access, and sandboxed execution without requiring human-in-the-loop per turn. Agents triggerable by API, webhook, or cron. 1.0 Beta adds database adapters (MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Supabase), image inputs, npm-importable skills, and new CLI commands. Deploys to Node.js or Cloudflare Workers. Model-agnostic.
caveats: Blog publication date not displayed; June 16 date from search indexing
GitHub stars count as of June 18 run

items.title: Anthropic Opens Seoul Office with Six Enterprise Partnerships
items.summary: Anthropic's Seoul office opened June 17 (third APAC location, after Tokyo and Bengaluru). KiYoung Choi as Representative Director. Six enterprise deployments: NAVER (Claude Code, full engineering org), Samsung SDS (Claude Cowork + Claude Code, Samsung Electronics), LG CNS (enterprise-wide software dev and client solutions), Nexon (live-service game dev), Hanwha Solutions (Claude via AWS Bedrock, in-region data), Channel Corp (Claude in Channel Talk, 230k+ businesses). 60 NAIRL consortium researcher seats. At June 17 launch briefing, head of international Chaoui said Fable 5 export controls expected to ease within days — not confirmed by US government.
caveats: Chaoui 'expected to ease within days' claim from DigitalToday.co.kr (T3) attributed to June 17 press briefing; not confirmed by Commerce Department
Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain unavailable to foreign nationals as of June 18

items.title: Claude Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 Model IDs Retired on June 15
items.summary: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and claude-opus-4-20250514 retired June 15, 2026 at 9AM PT. API calls now return errors; no auto-failover. Recommended replacements: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-8. Deprecation announced April 14 (62 days notice). Also noted: claude-opus-4-1-20250805 deprecated June 5 (retirement August 5); claude-mythos-preview retires June 30. Note: temperature, top_p, top_k deprecated on Opus 4.7+ — set to non-default values returns 400 error.

items.title: Gemini CLI Stops Serving on June 18 for Individual and Pro Users
items.summary: June 18 is the hard shutdown date for Gemini CLI on Google AI Pro, Google AI Ultra, and free individual Gemini Code Assist users. Replacement: Antigravity CLI (Go-based, async multi-agent). Feature mapping: Agent Skills → carry over, Hooks → carry over, Subagents → carry over, Extensions → Antigravity plugins. Google cannot guarantee 1:1 feature parity at launch. Enterprise (Standard/Enterprise license) and GitHub Gemini Code Assist organizations unaffected.
key_claims: Deadline: June 18, 2026 — AI Pro/Ultra and individual tier
Main architectural change: async multi-agent parallel sessions

items.title: G7 Leaders Discuss 'Trusted Partners' Framework for US AI Model Access
items.summary: At the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France (June 16–18), US Commerce Secretary Lutnick proposed a 'trusted partners' framework giving vetted allied-nation organizations access to restricted US AI models. Three diplomatic sources cited by Reuters. Framework would create tiered access based on safety, transparency, accountability criteria. Direct response to the June 12 directive blocking foreign national access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Altman (OpenAI), Amodei (Anthropic), Hassabis (DeepMind) attended June 17 summit lunch. No formal document published as of June 18.
caveats: Relies on three unnamed diplomatic sources — no formal government document
CNBC and Japan Times primary articles paywalled (403/402) in this run; T3 set accordingly
Framework criteria, process, and timeline not published
